name: Mailgun MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Mailgun MCP server enabling AI agents to send transactional emails, manage mailing lists, query email logs, validate email addresses, and access Mailgun's deliverability analytics.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails (confirmations, notifications) from workflow agents
Querying email delivery logs and bounce rates from analytics agents
Validating email addresses before sending from data quality agents
Managing mailing lists and subscribers from CRM agents
Scheduling bulk email campaigns from marketing automation agents
Monitoring deliverability metrics and spam complaints from ops agents
not_for: Teams using SendGrid, Postmark, SES, or other email providers
Marketing-heavy email campaigns (use Klaviyo or Mailchimp for that)
Two-way email threads or inbox management
best_when: An agent needs to send transactional emails or manage Mailgun's email delivery — especially for developer-first email infrastructure.
avoid_when: You're using SendGrid, AWS SES, or another email provider.

auth.notes: Mailgun API key with domain-level access. Private API key for full access; public key for email validation only. No fine-grained scope control.

pricing.paid_tiers: Flex: from $15/month for 5K emails; Scale: custom
pricing.notes: Usage-based pricing per email sent. Free trial available. Good value for transactional email at scale. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API: 60 requests/minute; email sending limits by plan

known_agent_gotchas: Domain must be verified (DNS records) before sending — setup required upfront
EU vs US API endpoints are different — use correct region
API key has no scope granularity — full account access
Bounce and unsubscribe handling is automatic — understand suppression lists
Stored messages in logs have limited retention (30 days default)
Email validation API is separate from sending API — different key type
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key lacks scope granularity. SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA. Domain verification required for sending.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key lacks scopes. SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA. Domain verification prevents spoofing.
